rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts To Respond To Disovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JULES RADCLIFFE, MICHAEL JELLISON Motion To Compel Defendants To Respond To Discovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to November 14, 2014 on the Court's own Motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TWIN CITY FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WELLS FARGO BANK LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions Against Pltff In The Sum Of $3396.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 3, Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff In The Sum Of $3396.00. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff is ordered to appear for the second session of her deposition on or before November 28, 2014. Defendant shall serve this order and a notice of deposition by hand, or, if 3 hand-service attempts are not successful, by overnight mail. If plaintiff does not appear for her deposition on or before November 28, 2014, plaintiff's case will be dismissed on December 12, 2014. Defendant is ordered to file and serve a declaration of service of the notice of deposition and state whether plaintiff appeared for the second session of her deposition by December 5, 2014. The Court will dismiss the action on December 12, 2014 if plaintiff does not appear. Sanctions in the amount of $700 are awarded against plaintiff. All outstanding sanctions shall be paid by plaintiff to defendant's counsel by November 28, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. EDWARD OOLSHANETSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Entirety Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS EDWARD OLSHANETSKY, BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion To Strike The Entirety Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint. Denied. Although Plaintiff did not file her amended complaint within 10 days, it serves judicial economy to allow the amended complaint to stand. If the complaint were stricken, Plaintiff could file a motion for leave to file an amended complaint, which would almost certainly be granted given California's liberal policy towards amendments and the fact that the trial date has not been set. Defendants have not shown any prejudice from the minor delay in the filing of the amended complaint. Additionally, there is no basis to dismiss the complaint, as the demurrer was only sustained as to one cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC VS. DEEM INC. (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied in its entirety. Deem failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden of production with regard to Access's complaint based on Access's discovery responses. Those responses are unverified and tantamount to no response at all. See Appleton v. Sup. Crt., (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636; Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Sup. Crt., (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651, 657. Deem did, however, shift the burden based on the declaration of Kristina Taylor. Ms. Taylor declares that Access failed to perform the contract. (Taylor dec., par. 11). In opposition, the declaration of Brian Regan creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Access performed the contract. The court overrules Deem's objection to that declaration. The motion is denied as to Deem's cross-complaint. First, the unverified discovery responses that Deem relies upon does not shift the burden as explained above. Second, Deem at most has shown that Access does not have facts to support its defenses. Form Interrogatory 15.1 asks for facts regarding Access's defenses. To maintain its initial summary judgment/summary adjudication burden on the cross-complaint, Deem needs to present evidence to establish the elements of each of its causes of action. See CCP 437c(p)(1). Deem failed to do so in this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON CHOY INDIVIDUALLY ET AL VS. DC DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTS DC DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC, DAVID CHANG, PAOLIN CHANG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction, res judicata, and collateral estoppel, as none of these doctrines bar this action. First, both actions are pending in the same superior court and the Court in the action brought by Defendant for administrative mandamus lacks the power to litigate all of the issues and grant all of the relief sought in this action. Second, there has been no final judgment issued in the other pending action. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the allegations supporting a theory of alter ego liability. Plaintiff concedes that the First Amended Complaint (FAC) fails to allege an unjust result, which is a required element for alter ego liability, and the cases relied upon by Plaintiff were presented in a different procedural posture than this case and are not compelling authority here. When the court is asked to take some action upon an alter ego theory at the pleadings stage, more is required than was pleaded here. (See, e.g., Norins Realty Co. v. Consolidated Abstract & Title Guaranty Co. (1947) 80 Cal.App.2d 879.) The Demurrer is sustained as to the breach of contract cause of action. While the FAC sufficiently states the elements of the cause of action, in light of the failure to sufficiently allege alter ego liability, Plaintiff has not alleged a cause of action against the individual defendants. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend to do so. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence. The FAC fails to state a claim distinct from the breach of contract claim. Next, the Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. Plaintiff concedes that the FAC fails to allege required elements. Additionally, justifiable reliance must be specifically pled. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained as to the intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend in order to allege where Defendant David Chang made the alleged misrepresentations; and how, where, to whom and by what means the alleged misrepresentations were made by Defendant Paolin Chang. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE W THORPE VS. KOROSH SHAKERI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Plaintiff To Have A Jury Trial

rulings.Case Title: CHAITANYA RAMAVAJJALA VS. ANANYA PATTANAYAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT ANANYA PATTANAYAK'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Default/Default Judgment is granted. Defendant has moved for relief within a reasonable time, provided a proposed pleading, and demonstrated excusable neglect/mistake to warrant relief under CCP 473. Defendant, who was acting in pro per, was reasonably confused as to the response date for the complaint. The subsequently served discovery request provided for a subsequent response date and it was not unreasonable for defendant to confuse the two response dates. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IGOR KRAYCHIK VS. EXIGEN (USA), INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 7, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC., EXIGEN ELITE, INC, EXIGEN MORTAGE ASSET MANAGEMENT, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is granted pursuant to CCP 473. The default entered on September 16, 2014 is vacated. Defendants have established grounds for relief arising from their mistake as to material facts related to their duty to respond and/or miscalendaring. There was confusion regarding the date of service. There was also uncertainty arising from the change in the name of defendant Exigen Insurance Solutions, Inc. to EIS Group, Inc. Defendants have moved promptly for relief and California has a strong policy of having cases resolved on the merits. Defendants shall file responsive pleadings within 15 days from entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
